Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The text supports that overexpression decreases folate content, and it does not report major growth penalties for GGH suppression in the cited studies.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Table 1 specifically lists AtGGH2 and LeGGH2 in engineering examples.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The review explicitly names pterin aldehyde reductase (PTAR).

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The review says one Arabidopsis isoform belongs to the short-chain dehydrogenase/reductase family.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: PTAR is discussed under folate salvage processes.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: PTAR specifically functions in pterin salvage.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: PTAR is clearly described as part of salvage/recycling, not de novo biosynthesis.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The paper explicitly states PTAR isoforms can reduce both dihydropterin-6-aldehyde and pterin-6-aldehyde.

Primary_Product.reason: The review explicitly states reduction of dihydropterin-6-aldehyde gives HMDHP.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The text directly describes reduction of pterin aldehydes to enable salvage back into biosynthesis.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: PTAR supplies the precursor HMDHP back into folate synthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: PTAR contributes to recycling material back to folate synthesis.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: The paper says the reaction appears to take place predominantly in the cytosol in pea leaves.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The review states PTAR salvage activity prevents irreversible oxidation and that high combined activity likely favors rapid conversion to HMDHP.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Multiple NADPH-dependent isoforms, one cloned Arabidopsis SDR-family isoform, and much higher activity than de novo enzymes are all explicitly stated.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: The review cites in vivo salvage, enzyme assays, cloned isoform characterization, and knockout evidence for one isoform.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The review explicitly concludes that broad-specificity aldehyde reductases rather than a single dedicated enzyme seem responsible.
